pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Itsy’s Soft Bridge, page 1

readAloudText.Wonder: Itsy finds a little stream shining across the path where everyone wants to cross.
readAloudText.Explore: Itsy reached the path first and stopped. A little stream crossed the way, silver and splashy under the evening sky.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Itsy’s Soft Bridge, page 2

readAloudText.Wonder: Daisy can hop across, but she notices Pip does not want to jump.
readAloudText.Explore: Daisy could hop to the other side, but she saw Pip’s paws stay still. His ears tilted back, and his tail wrapped around his feet.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Itsy’s Soft Bridge, page 3

readAloudText.Wonder: Dino offers to step in, then worries his big foot will splash everyone.
readAloudText.Explore: Dino lifted one big gentle foot. Then he lowered it again. “I might make a sploosh too big,” he said.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Itsy’s Soft Bridge, page 4

readAloudText.Wonder: Itsy studies leaves, reeds, and moss like a tiny bridge builder.
readAloudText.Explore: Itsy tapped his chin. He looked at a leaf, a reed, a cushion of moss, and a smooth little stick. “A soft bridge,” he said.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Itsy’s Soft Bridge, page 5

readAloudText.Wonder: The friends collect only fallen leaves, reeds, and moss from the ground.
readAloudText.Explore: Daisy found a fallen leaf. Pip found a reed already lying down. Dino brought moss that had rolled loose from a stone.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Itsy’s Soft Bridge, page 6

readAloudText.Wonder: Itsy shows how to lay the first leaf flat for tiny gentle feet.
readAloudText.Explore: Itsy placed the first leaf on the water. It floated like a little green boat, then settled beside the bank.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Itsy’s Soft Bridge, page 7

readAloudText.Wonder: Daisy adds a second leaf, matching its edge to Itsy’s first leaf.
readAloudText.Explore: Daisy held her leaf carefully and slid it beside Itsy’s. The edges touched like two friends holding hands.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Itsy’s Soft Bridge, page 8

readAloudText.Wonder: Pip adds a reed rail so the bridge feels safer.
readAloudText.Explore: Pip nudged a reed along the side. It made a tiny rail, just enough for a small paw to trust.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Itsy’s Soft Bridge, page 9

readAloudText.Drift: Dino adds moss at the end.
readAloudText.Wonder: Dino places moss at the far end so everyone can land softly.
readAloudText.Explore: Dino used one careful claw to set moss on the other bank. “For soft landings,” he whispered.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Itsy’s Soft Bridge, page 10

readAloudText.Wonder: The soft bridge wobbles once, and everyone pauses instead of rushing.
readAloudText.Explore: The bridge went wobble. Everyone froze. No one shouted. They waited until the water became smooth again.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Itsy’s Soft Bridge, page 11

readAloudText.Wonder: Itsy crosses first with tiny careful steps and a proud little smile.
readAloudText.Explore: Itsy stepped leaf, leaf, reed, moss. He reached the other side and took a tiny bow.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Itsy’s Soft Bridge, page 12

readAloudText.Wonder: Pip crosses next while Daisy hums a steady tune.
readAloudText.Explore: Pip listened to Daisy’s humming. Step. Hum. Step. Hum. Soon all four paws were safe on the moss.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Itsy’s Soft Bridge, page 13

readAloudText.Wonder: Dino crosses last, moving so slowly that the bridge barely ripples.
readAloudText.Explore: Dino crossed with careful toes. The stream made only a tiny shiver, and Dino looked very proud of his quiet feet.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Itsy’s Soft Bridge, page 14

readAloudText.Drift: They leave the bridge for others.
readAloudText.Wonder: The friends look back and see the bridge floating softly for the next small traveler.
readAloudText.Explore: On the other side, they turned around. The bridge waited there, soft and kind, in case another little traveler came along.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Itsy’s Soft Bridge, page 15

readAloudText.Wonder: Itsy curls up happily, knowing small ideas can carry big friends.
readAloudText.Explore: Itsy curled beside his friends as the stars came out. His idea had been small, but it had carried everyone gently home.
